response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, and he shows no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience, nor any SQL Server or Oracle background. The fintech-adjacent stint at RIDE Capital was JavaScript-based and does not substitute for capital-markets domain knowledge paired with C++.
strengths: Experience building low-latency, high-throughput backend systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling
Broad polyglot background with some professional C/C++ systems work and community teaching experience
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
